# @zakkster/lite-headless / hover-card

A rich hover/focus preview card (think a GitHub user hovercard). The floating
positioning is driven by **@zakkster/lite-floating** (createFloating +
bindTransform + offset/flip/shift), whose autoUpdate pulls in
**@zakkster/lite-observe** transitively to keep the card pinned on scroll and
resize.

This is the one primitive that uses the standalone floating engine instead of
the shared `_overlay/position` positioner -- a deliberate showcase of the
lite-floating integration. The other overlay primitives keep `_overlay/position`
to preserve their exact-pixel test contracts.

## Quick example

```js
import { createHoverCard } from "@zakkster/lite-headless/hover-card";

const hc = createHoverCard({
    placement: "bottom-start",
    openDelay: 300,
    closeDelay: 200,
});
hc.attachTrigger(linkEl);
hc.attachContent(cardEl);   // portaled, positioned, aria-hidden while closed
```

The pointer is considered "alive" while it is over the trigger **or** the card,
so the user can move from the link into the card without it closing.

## Options

| Option | Default | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| `placement` | `"bottom"` | Floating-UI placement string (e.g. `"top-end"`) |
| `offset` | `8` | gap in px between trigger and card |
| `flip` | `true` | flip to the opposite side when it would overflow |
| `shift` | `true` | shift along the axis to stay in view |
| `openDelay` | `300` | ms hover-intent before opening (focus opens instantly) |
| `closeDelay` | `200` | ms grace period for crossing the trigger->card gap |
| `closeOnEscape` | `true` | Escape closes the card |
| `container` | `document.body` | portal target |
| `transition` | `false` | wait for `transitionend` before flipping status |

## API

```ts
{
    open(): boolean
    status(): "closed" | "opening" | "open" | "closing"
    setOpen(open: boolean, reason?: string): void
    toggle(reason?: string): void

    attachRoot(el): off
    attachTrigger(el): off
    attachAnchor(el): off       // position relative to a different element
    attachContent(el): off
    attachArrow(el): off

    destroy(): void
}
```

## Painted attributes

- trigger: `data-hover-card-trigger`, `data-open`, `aria-expanded` (no aria-haspopup)
- content: `data-hover-card-content`, `data-open`, `data-status`, `data-placement` ("bottom-start"), `data-side` (top/right/bottom/left), `data-align` (start/center/end), `aria-hidden` while closed, `tabindex="-1"`
- arrow: `data-side`
- root: `data-hover-card-root`, `data-open`

A hover card is a supplementary, sighted-user affordance: the content carries
**no role** and is `aria-hidden` while closed, so assistive tech skips it while
the trigger link still carries the real destination.

## Wrapper (`<lite-hover-card>`)

```html
<lite-hover-card placement="bottom-start" open-delay="300">
  <a data-trigger href="/u/zak">@zak</a>
  <div data-content>
    <div data-arrow></div>
    <strong>Zahary Shinikchiev</strong>
    <p>Author of the @zakkster/* zero-GC ecosystem.</p>
  </div>
</lite-hover-card>
```

Attributes: `placement`, `offset`, `open-delay`, `close-delay`, `no-escape`,
`container`, `transition`. Host: `toggle()`, `setOpen(v)`, `isOpen`, `status`.

## Composition

Pairs naturally with `avatar`, `badge`, and `card` inside the content. Use
`attachAnchor` to anchor the card to a wrapper while the hover target is a
smaller inline element.
